title: MCP server — Communications
description: Tool surface for the canonical Communications MCP server. Stages outreach for workspace-mediated user confirmation; does NOT directly launch transports (AC-MCP-B). The server returns a mailto: URL plus a full payload preview; the user's mail client — acting as the workspace for this outreach — opens the URL with the composition pre-populated, and the user reviews and clicks send. AC-18 (transport eligibility): mailto: passes — the mechanism can't read message content, and the downstream mail client is the user's choice. AC-19 (user-visible payload before send): the `preview` field exposes recipients + subject + body explicitly so the AI client can show the full composition to the user before they open the link. State posture: staged compositions live in process memory only; nothing on disk, nothing across restarts. See spec/PNA_Spec.md § Universal architectural commitments (AC-MCP-B, AC-18, AC-19) and § Vision (canonical MCP servers).

server_name.description: Stable identifier the AI client wires up in its MCP config.

transport.description: JSON-RPC frames over stdin/stdout, per the MCP transport contract for desktop AI clients.

tool_stage_email.description: Build a mailto: URL plus a payload preview from a draft composition. Returns a staging_id, the URL, and a warnings list for issues the AI client should surface to the user (URL-length problems, no recipients, bcc-vs-to recommendations on group sends). Recipients are de-duplicated case-insensitively; empty addresses are dropped. Does NOT send: opening the URL is what hands the composition off to the user's mail client.

subject.description: Email subject. Empty string is allowed.

body.description: Email body. Empty string is allowed.

to.description: Visible primary recipients. May be empty or null for BCC-only group sends.

tool_get_staged.description: Look up a previously staged composition by id. Returns null when the id isn't known (process restart, eviction past the in-memory cap, or never staged).

StagedComposition.description: Shape returned by stage_email and get_staged. The `preview` field is load-bearing for AC-19: an AI client uses it to show the full composition to the user before opening the mailto: URL.

staging_id.description: Opaque id; pass back to get_staged to recall this composition.

mailto_url.description: RFC 6068 mailto: URL with subject, body, cc, bcc percent-encoded as URL parameters. Opening the URL hands the composition to the user's mail client.

warnings.description: Human-readable issues the AI client should surface to the user before they open the mailto: URL — e.g., URL exceeds the conservative ~2 KB ceiling some mail clients enforce, or multiple recipients in `to` (consider BCC for privacy).

Preview.description: Full payload preview, exposed explicitly so AC-19 (user-visible payload before send) can be honored at the workspace boundary.

url_byte_length.description: UTF-8 byte length of `mailto_url`. The conservative warn threshold is ~2 KB (some mail clients — Outlook, Gmail-via-mailto — start truncating beyond that).
